title: selaginella strigosa
intro: Selaginella strigosa, also known as the striped spikemoss, is a species of spikemoss that belongs to the family Selaginellaceae, a group of small, non-vascular plants that thrive in tropical and subtropical regions. Traditionally, Selaginella strigosa has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The plant is believed to offer several health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and soothing skin irritations, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies. In herbal preparations, Selaginella strigosa is often used in teas, infusions, and poultices to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is necessary to confirm its efficacy and safety.
